features.name: Contract system
features.description: Zod-validated Workflow/Item/Action contract that drives all generic UI rendering. Single source of truth — workflows are data, not code.

features.name: Contract-driven dashboard, queue & flyout
features.description: Generic shell renders any workflow's queue (table/cards), detail flyout (source vs proposed panes), bulk actions and hotkeys purely from the contract.

features.name: Authentication & route guard
features.description: Better Auth email/password session plus cookie-based middleware guard redirecting unauthenticated users.

features.name: Database schema & unified seed
features.description: Drizzle schema for workflows + workflow_items alongside Better Auth tables, with one-command idempotent seed (admin user + domain tables + workflows/items).
features.evidence: db/schema.ts, db/index.ts, scripts/seed.ts; run `npx tsx scripts/seed.ts` (needs DATABASE_URL)

features.name: Inbound webhook ingestion
features.description: POST /api/workflows/[id]/items endpoint: HMAC-authenticated, ItemSchema-validated, idempotent on item.id; external engines enqueue and settle items.

features.name: Outbound decision dispatch
features.description: Reviewer decisions persist immediately as `dispatching`, then fire an HMAC-signed outbound POST to the action handler URL, validated against a per-workflow SSRF allowlist.

features.name: Green the baseline lint gate
features.description: `./init.sh` currently fails on 3 pre-existing eslint errors: react-hooks/set-state-in-effect in app/dashboard/DashboardClient.tsx:219 and app/data/DataClient.tsx:144, and react/jsx-no-comment-textnodes in app/data/DataClient.tsx:187. `npx tsc --noEmit` is clean. Resolve these (the set-state-in-effect effects intentionally re-sync on prop change — verify behavior is preserved) so the baseline gate passes.
features.evidence: Replaced useEffect+setState with computed-during-render prev-ref pattern in DashboardClient.tsx and DataClient.tsx; fixed JSX comment-text-nodes; `./init.sh` exits 0 (tsc + lint clean)

features.name: Unit testing
features.description: Stand up Vitest for fast, offline unit tests of pure logic: contract Zod schemas and helpers (resolveItemActions, bulkActions, singleActions, intentToVariant in lib/contract/index.ts), formatters (lib/format.ts), and the renderField registry (lib/renderField.tsx). HARNESS WIRING (part of done): add a `test` script to package.json, add `npm test` to init.sh's offline gate, and document it under 'Verification Commands' in CLAUDE.md. Closes the audit's 'Test command documented' gap.
features.evidence: vitest.config.ts + lib/contract/index.test.ts, lib/format.test.ts, lib/renderField.test.tsx (44 tests); `npm test` (TZ=UTC vitest run) added to package.json + init.sh offline gate + CLAUDE.md Verification Commands; ./init.sh exits 0

features.name: Integration testing
features.description: Integration tests for module boundaries that touch I/O, reusing the Vitest runner from feat-008: inbound webhook route handler (HMAC auth + ItemSchema validation + idempotency on item.id, app/api/workflows/[id]/items/route.ts) and outbound decision dispatch server action (dispatching status + SSRF allowlist, app/actions/decisions.ts), against a mocked/in-memory DB via the db/index.ts getDb() seam. HARNESS WIRING (part of done): add a `test:integration` script, add it to the init.sh gate, and document it in CLAUDE.md Verification Commands.
features.evidence: tests/integration/{helpers.ts,inbound-route.test.ts,decisions.test.ts} + vitest.integration.config.ts (25 tests) against in-memory PGlite via vi.mock('@/db'); `npm run test:integration` (TZ=UTC vitest run --config vitest.integration.config.ts) added to package.json + init.sh offline gate + CLAUDE.md Verification Commands; ./init.sh exits 0 (44 unit + 25 integration)

features.name: E2E testing
features.description: Playwright end-to-end coverage of the critical path: login -> dashboard queue -> open detail flyout -> fire a decision (approve/reject) -> verify the item's status changes. Requires a running app + seeded DB, so it is a SEPARATE heavier gate, NOT part of the offline ./init.sh. HARNESS WIRING (part of done): add a `test:e2e` script + playwright config, document it in CLAUDE.md Verification Commands as the manual/heavier gate (with its DB + browser prerequisites), and note in progress.md that it is excluded from the offline gate.

features.name: FastAPI unit testing
features.description: pytest unit tests for pure logic in the FastAPI data API (api/): Pydantic request/response schema validation (api/models/schemas.py), settings/config loading (api/config.py), and any validation/computed helpers. No database required. HARNESS WIRING (part of done): add pytest (+ pytest-asyncio) to api/pyproject.toml dev dependencies, create api/tests/, and document the command `cd api && uv run pytest` under 'Verification Commands' in CLAUDE.md. Note: the FastAPI service is a separate Python sub-project, so this does NOT go in the npm-based root init.sh offline gate.

features.name: FastAPI integration (API) testing
features.description: pytest integration tests exercising the routers end-to-end via httpx ASGITransport against a disposable test Postgres: domain CRUD (shippers/carriers/lanes/loads/rate-snapshots), HITL queue (POST /workflow-items idempotent on id, PATCH /workflow-items/{id} settle), workflow query shortcuts (quote-desk pending, shipper-reactivation dormant, carrier-reconciliation discrepancies), /health, and 404/validation error paths. Override the db.session engine with a test DATABASE_URL (asyncpg scheme). HARNESS WIRING (part of done): requires a test Postgres, so it is a SEPARATE heavier gate (not the offline one); run via `cd api && uv run pytest` and document it plus its DB prerequisite in CLAUDE.md Verification Commands.

features.name: FastAPI migration & schema parity testing
features.description: pytest tests that Alembic `upgrade head` (and downgrade) apply cleanly on a fresh test DB, and that the three schema sources stay in agreement: SQLAlchemy models (api/db/models.py), the SQL migration (api/db/migrations/001_initial_schema.sql), and the Alembic version (api/alembic/versions/001_initial_schema.py). Cover computed columns (loads.gross_margin must never be settable directly) and confirm the workflow_items shape matches what the Next.js Drizzle layer (db/schema.ts) consumes. HARNESS WIRING (part of done): runs under the same `cd api && uv run pytest` command (scoped by a tests subdir/marker), needs a test DB, documented in CLAUDE.md Verification Commands.

features.name: trigger.dev scaffolding & AI SDK/OpenRouter wiring
features.description: Stand up trigger.dev in the repo (the engine side of the already-seeded quote-desk contract): `npx trigger.dev@latest init` → trigger.config.ts + trigger/ dir, add @trigger.dev/sdk + ai (Vercel AI SDK) + @openrouter/ai-sdk-provider to package.json, and env scaffolding (TRIGGER_PROJECT_REF/SECRET_KEY/ACCESS_TOKEN, OPENROUTER_API_KEY, DATA_API_BASE_URL) in .env.example. Establish two shared helpers used by the quote-desk tasks: (1) an AI SDK model factory wrapping the OpenRouter provider (default anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6, swappable by model id), and (2) a typed FastAPI data-API client so tasks write workflow_items via the hitl router (per api/db/models.py header), NOT the Next.js inbound webhook. HARNESS WIRING (part of done): document `npx trigger.dev@latest dev`/`deploy` under CLAUDE.md Verification Commands and note it is NOT part of the offline ./init.sh gate (needs trigger.dev creds); seed the docs/clients/halberd-co/workflows/quote-desk-setup.md infra doc. Verify with `npx tsc --noEmit` (offline) clean.
features.evidence: trigger.config.ts (project ref from TRIGGER_PROJECT_REF, dirs ./trigger) + trigger/ dir (lib/ai.ts model factory wrapping @openrouter/ai-sdk-provider, default anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6 swappable; lib/data-api.ts typed FastAPI hitl-router client mirroring api/models/schemas.py; example.ts quote-desk-health scaffold task). Deps @trigger.dev/sdk@^4 + ai@^6 + @openrouter/ai-sdk-provider@^2 added. .env.example: TRIGGER_PROJECT_REF/SECRET_KEY/ACCESS_TOKEN, OPENROUTER_API_KEY, DATA_API_BASE_URL(+TOKEN); .trigger gitignored. Docs: quote-desk-setup.md updated + CLAUDE.md Verification Commands note trigger dev/deploy NOT in offline gate. Offline gate green: npx tsc --noEmit clean, lint + 44 unit + 25 integration pass.

features.name: Quote Desk: Gmail RFQ intake + LLM structured extraction
features.description: trigger.dev task that turns an inbound RFQ at quotes@halberd-co.com into a validated structured object. Real-time intake path: Gmail API users.watch → Google Pub/Sub topic → push subscription → trigger.dev HTTP-trigger task (document a scheduled poll fallback). The task fetches the message, then extracts structured RFQ JSON — origin, destination, equipment (map free text / 'dry van|reefer|flatbed' ↔ lane equipment_code V/R/F/SD/DD), pickup date, weight/commodity, accessorials — using the AI SDK generateObject with a Zod RfqSchema via OpenRouter (Claude default from feat-014). Emits a validated RFQ payload consumed by feat-017. Append the Gmail API enablement, OAuth/domain-wide-delegation, and Pub/Sub topic+subscription+push-URL steps to quote-desk-setup.md. Not in the offline gate; the Zod schema + equipment-mapping + parser helpers should be unit-testable offline (Vitest).
features.evidence: trigger/lib/rfq.ts (Zod RfqSchema + normalizeEquipment free-text↔V/R/F/SD/DD + parseWeight/formatLane/reconcileEquipment + buildExtractionPrompt) and trigger/lib/gmail.ts (parsePubSubPush envelope decode, parseGmailMessage/extractPlainText/getHeader, thin GmailClient). trigger/quote-desk-intake.ts: quote-desk-intake task (list added msgs → fetch → generateObject w/ RfqSchema over OpenRouter → emit RfqPayload[] for feat-017) + quote-desk-poll scheduled fallback. Push URL app/api/gmail/push (PUBSUB_VERIFICATION_TOKEN-verified → tasks.trigger). vitest.config.ts include extended to trigger/**/*.test.ts; rfq.test.ts + gmail.test.ts add 30 unit tests (74 total). .env.example + quote-desk-setup.md §4 (Gmail API/OAuth/Pub-Sub/watch steps) + CLAUDE.md updated. Offline gate green: npx tsc --noEmit clean, lint clean, 74 unit + 25 integration pass. Not in offline gate: live Gmail+LLM path (needs GMAIL_ACCESS_TOKEN + OPENROUTER_API_KEY).

features.name: FastAPI RateInsights endpoint (mock Truckstop.com RateInsights)
features.description: Add a RateInsights rate-estimate endpoint to the FastAPI data API that approximates Truckstop.com's RateInsights API shape from the lanes + rate_snapshots tables. Given origin/destination (city/state, optionally zip), equipment_code, and optional pickup date, derive an estimate: low/avg/high rate-per-mile, mileage, total all-in band, fuel surcharge, and a confidence + comparable-count score. Matching logic: same lane + equipment within a recent window from rate_snapshots, falling back to lane aggregates (avg_*_rate_per_mile) and then nearby/looser matches; thin history → low confidence. Pydantic request/response models mirroring RateInsights' fields, new router wired in main.py, optionally persisting the lookup as a rate_snapshot (rate_source='truckstop'). HARNESS WIRING: the pure matching/aggregation helper is offline unit-testable and folds into the api/ pytest gate (feat-011/012); document the endpoint in docs/API.md. Independent of the trigger.dev work.
features.evidence: Pure helper api/services/rate_insights.py (estimate_rate: tiers lane_snapshots→lane_aggregate→loose_snapshots→none over a 90-day window; low/avg/high rate-per-mile + fuel + all-in total band; confidence_score/level scaled by comparable count, high≥0.75 aligned to quote-desk confidenceFloor) — no DB/FastAPI imports, offline-testable. Pydantic RateInsightsRequest/RateInsightsEstimateOut in api/models/schemas.py. Router api/routers/rate_insights.py (POST /rate-insights/estimate, optional ?persist=true writing a rate_source='truckstop' snapshot) wired in api/main.py. 10 offline unit tests in api/tests/test_rate_insights.py — `cd api && PYTHONPATH=. uv run --with pytest pytest tests/test_rate_insights.py` → 10 passed (these fold into the feat-011/012 pytest gate). Endpoint documented in docs/API.md. main.py imports + route wiring verified; py_compile clean. npm offline gate (./init.sh) still green: tsc + lint + 44 unit + 25 integration.

features.name: Quote Desk: draft generation, queue write & send-on-approval
features.description: Close the quote-desk loop per docs/clients/halberd-co/workflows/quote-desk.md. Consume the extracted RFQ (feat-015), call the FastAPI RateInsights endpoint (feat-016) for a rate band, and draft the quote reply in Halberd's voice (greeting, all-in target rate, transit estimate, validity window, booking ask) via the AI SDK over OpenRouter. Score confidence against confidenceFloor 0.75 — above it the draft is auto-pass eligible, below it the item holds pending for a broker (thin/odd lanes suppress the number). Write a quote-desk workflow_item via the FastAPI hitl router: summary, fields {lane, equipment, pickup, rate, score}, sourceContent = RFQ + comparable-lane evidence, proposedOutput = the draft, context refs for the comps. On 'Approve & send' (and 'Adjust rate & send') the existing outbound decision dispatch (feat-006) fires the quote email back to the shipper via the Gmail send API (threaded onto the RFQ) and marks the load quoted. Append Gmail send scope/sender setup to quote-desk-setup.md. Pure draft/confidence/item-mapping helpers unit-testable offline; the send path is an integration concern.
